data.title: ANTA Sports Delivers Double-Digit Profit and Revenue Growth in 2025
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ANTA Sports Products ( (HK:2020) ) has provided an update.

ANTA Sports reported audited 2025 results showing revenue rose 13.3% year on year to RMB80.2 billion, with overall operating profit margin improving 0.4 percentage points to 23.8% as it stepped up investment in branding, channels, product R&D and international expansion. Segment performance was mixed, with margin pressure at the core ANTA brand offset by an improved margin at FILA and slightly weaker profitability in other brands, while profit attributable to equity shareholders, excluding prior Amer Sports dilution gains, climbed 13.9% to RMB13.6 billion.

The group generated robust operating cash flow of RMB21.0 billion and free cash inflow of RMB16.1 billion, underscoring strong cash generation and balance sheet strength that enabled the board to propose a higher final dividend of HK108 cents per share for 2025, subject to shareholder approval. The annual results, which received an unqualified audit opinion from KPMG and were reviewed by the audit committee and board, reinforce ANTA’s positioning as a financially solid player in the sportswear sector, potentially supporting continued investment and shareholder returns amid ongoing industry competition.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2020) stock is a Hold with a HK$85.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ANTA Sports Products stock, see the HK:2020 Stock Forecast page.

More about ANTA Sports Products

ANTA Sports Products Limited is a leading Chinese sportswear company, listed in Hong Kong, that designs, manufactures and markets athletic footwear, apparel and accessories. Through its core ANTA brand, premium FILA line and a portfolio of other labels, the group targets mass‑market and higher-end consumers in China and is expanding its presence in overseas markets.

Average Trading Volume: 10,080,441

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: HK$209.4B

data.title: ABx Group Clarifies Data Sources in Amended Rare Earths PDAC Presentation
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ABx Group Limited ( (AU:ABX) ) has shared an update.

ABx Group has issued an amended version of its rare earths presentation prepared for the PDAC Convention in Toronto, updating key disclosure elements for investors. The revisions clarify data sourcing and peer comparison methods used in the presentation, potentially enhancing transparency and credibility around the company’s rare earth product composition and pricing assumptions.

The updated document now includes explicit references on slides outlining Mixed Rare Earth Carbonate composition and Rare Earth Oxide pricing to appendix data sources, which detail underlying datasets, peer selection methodology, and development stages. This refinement may help stakeholders better assess ABx’s competitive positioning in rare earth markets and evaluate the robustness of its technical and commercial assumptions.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:ABX) stock is a Hold with a A$0.08 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ABx Group Limited stock, see the AU:ABX Stock Forecast page.

More about ABx Group Limited

ABx Group Limited is an Australian company listed on the ASX that is focused on supplying light and heavy rare earths from projects in Tasmania. The company aims to feed Western supply chains with these critical minerals, positioning itself within the strategic rare earths sector supporting advanced manufacturing and clean energy technologies.

data.title: Total Metals Closes Menary Gold Project Acquisition in Key Ontario Mining District
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Total Metals ( (TSE:TT) ) has issued an announcement.

Total Metals Corp. has closed the acquisition of the Menary Gold Project in Ontario’s Kenora Mining Division, a 276.5-hectare package of 27 mining claims and one mining lease bordering the Rainy River land package recently tied to Coeur Mining’s multibillion-dollar takeover of New Gold. The property hosts multiple high-grade zones, including bonanza-grade grab samples, strong channel sampling results and a newly identified mineralized zone at depth, reinforcing the district’s status as a premier North American gold jurisdiction.

Under an option agreement, Total Metals can earn a 100% interest in Menary by paying $1,010,000 in cash and issuing an equal value in shares over four years, subject to a 4.5% net smelter royalty held by Western Troy Capital Corp. and William McNerney. The first tranche of consideration, totaling $210,000 in cash and 507,678 shares priced at $0.41365, has been completed on an arm’s length basis, signaling a meaningful expansion of Total Metals’ gold-focused portfolio in a highly active mining camp.

More about Total Metals

Total Metals Corp. is a Canadian exploration company focused on its 100% owned Electrolode Project in northwestern Ontario, targeting critical minerals and gold across three favorable geological trends near major Red Lake-area operations. The company also owns the High Lake and West Hawk Lake gold properties along the Manitoba–Ontario border, with the Purex Zone at High Lake prioritized for initial exploration and potential future mining.

Average Trading Volume: 232,970

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: C$17.08M

data.title: Nickel Industries Reports Contractor Fatality at ENC Project Site
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Nickel Mines Ltd. ( (AU:NIC) ) is now available.

Nickel Industries has reported a fatal accident involving a contractor working for PT Fajar Metal Industry on construction of transmission lines tied to the slurry plant and dry stacked tailings facility at its ENC project in Indonesia. The incident occurred on the Hengjaya Mine haul road, and the company has offered condolences to the worker’s family and colleagues while providing support.

The company is cooperating with Indonesia’s Department of Mines and Energy (ESDM) as authorities investigate the circumstances of the fatality. The event underscores the operational and safety risks inherent in large-scale mining infrastructure projects, and further updates are expected as inquiries progress.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:NIC) stock is a Buy with a A$1.95 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Nickel Mines Ltd. stock, see the AU:NIC Stock Forecast page.

More about Nickel Mines Ltd.

Nickel Industries Limited is an Australian-based resources company focused on nickel mining and downstream processing. Through operations such as the Hengjaya Mine and associated processing projects, it supplies nickel products into global stainless steel and battery materials supply chains.

Average Trading Volume: 16,132,056

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

IOUpay Limited ( (AU:OVT) ) has issued an update.

Ovanti Limited has notified the ASX that it is cancelling a previous Appendix 3B announcement relating to a proposed issue of securities. The company has withdrawn the remaining 256,666,667 securities that had been slated for issue under that proposal, in line with the contractual terms governing the arrangement.

The move alters Ovanti’s anticipated capital structure and suggests a change in its funding or issuance plans compared with what was outlined in June 2025. While the company has not detailed new financing alternatives in this notice, the cancellation means existing shareholders will face less dilution than originally expected under the prior proposal.

data.title: NZ Super Fund slips below 5% threshold in Summerset after share dilution
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from Summerset Group Holdings Limited ( (DE:20S) ) is now available.

The New Zealand Superannuation Fund, through its nominee New Zealand Superannuation Fund Nominees Limited, has ceased to be a substantial product holder in Summerset Group Holdings after its stake fell just below the 5% regulatory threshold. Despite a small increase in absolute shares held to 12,072,269, the fund’s percentage holding diluted to 4.976% as Summerset’s total shares on issue rose, driven by on-market trading activity and participation in a dividend reinvestment plan.

Between May 2025 and March 2026, the fund executed a series of on-market purchases and sales, buying about 1.45 million Summerset shares and selling roughly 1.53 million shares for similar aggregate values. It was also allotted more than 110,000 new shares under Summerset’s dividend reinvestment plan, with the overall changes prompting disclosure under New Zealand’s substantial product holder rules and signaling a marginal shift in the company’s institutional ownership base rather than a major strategic exit.

More about Summerset Group Holdings Limited

Summerset Group Holdings Limited is a New Zealand-based retirement village and aged-care operator that develops, owns, and manages integrated retirement villages. The company focuses on providing independent living, assisted living, and care services to older New Zealanders, positioning it as a significant player in the country’s retirement and aged-care property sector.

See more insights into 20S stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: FuelPositive Stays on Track Under Cease Trade Order as Farm Demand for Green Ammonia Grows
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from FuelPositive Corp. ( (TSE:NHHH) ).

FuelPositive reported that it remains under a management cease trade order after delaying the filing of audited financial statements for the year ended September 30, 2025, but emphasized that the restriction only affects trading by certain insiders while public trading continues. The company says it has adequate financial and human resources, is not in insolvency proceedings, and expects to complete the audit within the permitted timeframe while issuing mandated bi-weekly status updates until the order is lifted.

Alongside its reporting focus, FuelPositive continues to advance strategic and operational initiatives, including active talks with potential partners and growing engagement from farmers interested in its on-farm Green Ammonia systems as a hedge against supply chain risks and fertilizer price volatility. Management argues that current global conditions are reinforcing demand for decentralized, low-carbon fertilizer solutions, supporting the company’s ambition to position Canada as a centre of excellence for Green Ammonia production and to strengthen its standing in sustainable agriculture technology.

Spark’s Take on NHHH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NHHH is a Underperform.

FuelPositive Corp. faces substantial financial difficulties with no revenue and a high cash burn rate, significantly impacting its overall score. Technical indicators are mixed, with neutral momentum but potential overbought conditions. The valuation is poor due to negative earnings and no dividends, further lowering the score. Overall, the stock presents high risks with limited upside potential in its current financial state.

To see Spark’s full report on NHHH stock, click here.

More about FuelPositive Corp.

FuelPositive is a Canadian clean-tech company focused on decentralized, on-farm Green Ammonia production systems for agriculture. Its containerized units let farmers produce their own green nitrogen fertilizer and carbon-free fuel using sustainable electricity, aiming to reduce emissions, stabilize input costs, and cut reliance on volatile global fertilizer supply chains.

The company’s first FP300 demonstration system in Manitoba is designed to produce 100 metric tonnes of Green Ammonia annually, forming the basis for the larger FP1500 commercial model targeting roughly 500 metric tonnes for 10,000-acre farms. With operations in Ontario and Manitoba and support from engineering firm Stantec, FuelPositive plans to build a Canadian hub for decentralized Green Ammonia manufacturing, job creation, and food-system resilience.

Average Trading Volume: 124,187

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: C$24.78M

For a thorough assessment of NHHH stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Sunshine Metals Underpins North Queensland Projects With Detailed Resource Framework
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sunshine Gold Ltd ( (AU:SHN) ) has provided an update.

Sunshine Metals has outlined the technical foundations and resource framework for its Liontown and Sybil projects in North Queensland, highlighting the polymetallic nature of the deposits and the methodology used to report gold, copper and zinc equivalent grades. The release emphasises that the resource estimates and exploration results are underpinned by JORC-compliant Competent Person sign-offs for Liontown, Liontown East, Plateau, Waterloo, Orient and Sybil, reinforcing the credibility of its reported mineral resources and exploration data for investors and stakeholders.

By detailing metal price assumptions, metallurgical recoveries and calculation formulas for zinc equivalents, Sunshine Metals signals a structured approach to evaluating project economics and the potential for multi-commodity value recovery. This technical clarity, together with references to prior ASX releases on resource upgrades and consolidations, positions the company as methodical in de-risking its North Queensland portfolio and provides the market with a more transparent view of its exploration and resource-development strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:SHN) stock is a Sell with a A$0.02 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sunshine Gold Ltd stock, see the AU:SHN Stock Forecast page.

More about Sunshine Gold Ltd

Sunshine Metals Ltd, listed on the ASX under the ticker SHN, is an Australian mineral exploration and development company focused on gold and base metals. Its key assets include the Liontown and Sybil projects in North Queensland, where it is advancing multi-commodity systems containing gold, copper, zinc, lead and silver within several defined lodes and resource areas.
